projectScope.objective: Build palantir-mini Claude Code plugin (v2.1.108) + ~/.claude/schemas/ontology extension implementing OSDK 2.0 patterns and Palantir 5 must-include infra patterns (two-tier actions, edit functions returning Edits[] without commit, OSDK type codegen, 6-phase validation, submission criteria pre-flight) on append-only event log foundation, mirroring Anthropic Managed Agents Session/Harness/Sandbox virtualization, within Claude Code Native Runtime (API-Free, Claude Max X20, max v2.1.108 utilization). Cross-project: kosmos / palantir-math / mathcrew. Promote ~/.claude/research/palantir/ from declarative evidence to executable contract.
backendDomains: DATA — EventEnvelope (10-variant discriminated union), ObjectType, PropertyType (24 branded), SnapshotManifest, CommitSha (branded)

LOGIC — EditFunction (returns OntologyEdit[] without commit), InterfaceType, LinkType / Object-Backed LinkType, DerivedProperty, Reducer, LinkConstraint, SubmissionCriteria (computational), ValidationPhaseEvaluator (6-phase; v0 implements 3)
ACTION — Tier-1 Declarative Action, Tier-2 Function-Backed Action, AtomicCommit (fs.mkdir mutex), ActionExecutorAgent (Write/Edit allowlist), CodegenRun (descender regen)
SECURITY — Layer-1 RBAC Policy (managed-settings.d/ fragments), SubmissionCriteria (gate semantics), CapabilityToken (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/vault/)

LEARN — AppendOnlyEventLog (events.jsonl per project), DecisionLineage5Dim (WHEN/ATOP_WHICH/THROUGH_WHICH/BY_WHOM/WITH_WHAT), EvidenceSufficiency, LineageReplay (MCP tool replay_lineage), DriftSignal (drift_detected event variant)
frontendSurfaces: Claude Code CLI session surface — Hooks (24 events incl. PreCompact decision:block, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, PostToolUseFailure), Skills (namespaced /palantir-mini:*), Custom Subagents (.md frontmatter with tools allowlist + maxTurns + disallowedTools), Plugin Manifest (.claude-plugin/{plugin,marketplace}.json), MCP Bridge (bridge/mcp-server.ts as Harness API), managed-settings.d/ fragments per project

Code editor TypeScript surface — Generated descenders (src/generated/ per project), Branded RID types (ObjectTypeRid, LinkTypeRid, ActionTypeRid, etc.), Exhaustive visitor unions on EventEnvelope type tag

ddd: All 28 primitive names match Palantir / Anthropic Managed Agents / OSDK 2.0 domain vocabulary exactly: 'EditFunction', 'SubmissionCriteria', 'EventEnvelope', 'DecisionLineage', 'ObjectTypeRid', 'Tier-1/Tier-2 Action' — zero translation required across all 3 projects (kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew)
EventEnvelope 10-variant discriminated union uses Anthropic MA + Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim field names verbatim: when, atopWhich, throughWhich, byWhom, withWhat

Semantic heuristics SH-01/SH-02/SH-03/SH-04/SH-05 formalize domain classification: SH-02 (delete LOGIC+ACTION, still describes reality? -> DATA), SH-03 (returns Edits[]? -> LOGIC), SH-04 (governs WHO / under WHAT CONDITIONS? -> SECURITY), SH-05 (captures WHAT HAPPENED + WHY + LEARNED? -> LEARN)

Transition-zone classification rigidly applied: InterfaceType/LinkType/DerivedProperty/Reducer are LOGIC (not DATA) per ontologist authority; SubmissionCriteria is dual-modeled as LOGIC (computation) + SECURITY (gate) — both are intentional
dry: Single ontology declaration in ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ feeds codegen for all 3 projects — no per-project duplication of ObjectType/PropertyType/ActionType declarations
Single EventEnvelope discriminated union hosts all 10 event types without per-type envelope boilerplate; EventEnvelopeBase captures 5-dim lineage once, inherited by all variants
Single AtomicCommit primitive serves Tier-1 fast path (declarative CRUD) and Tier-2 slow path (function-backed) without per-type commit code
One replay_lineage MCP handler serves all backward propagation queries across all 3 projects; single foldToSnapshot Reducer derives current state from events.jsonl without per-mutation snapshot logic
palantir-math data.ts (141KB) anti-pattern explicitly cited as DRY violation to be refactored via OSDK 2.0 codegen + primitives/ split
ocp: Discriminated union pattern enables Open/Closed extension: new EventEnvelope variants add a new case, never modify existing variants — type exhaustiveness enforced at compile time via tsc
New ObjectTypes/LinkTypes/ActionTypes added as new declarations in schemas/ontology/primitives/ without modifying existing generator templates
New validation phase evaluators added to prim-logic-08 (3-phase -> 6-phase post-v0) without modifying the 3 existing v0 phase implementations
Codegen templates extended per new primitive category via new template file, never modified — OCP applied to the code generator itself
pecs: Producer Extends, Consumer Super applied at every generic boundary: EditFunction<Params, Edits extends OntologyEdit[]> where Edits is producer-extends; Action<F extends EditFunction<any,any>> where F extends
Reducer<In, Out>: In is consumer-super (accepts any event supertype), Out is producer-extends (produces typed snapshot subtype) — snapshot derivation from events.jsonl is the canonical PECS example
Replay<Filter, Event>: Filter is consumer-super (broad query accepts any filter subtype), Event is producer-extends (specific event subtype returned)
Exhaustive visitor on EventEnvelope type tag replaces switch-default anti-pattern; consumer code visits via supertype visitor, not per-variant switch blocks

primitives: EventEnvelope (DATA — discriminated union, 10 variants, base carries Decision Lineage 5-dim)
ObjectType (DATA — named entity with branded ObjectTypeRid)
PropertyType 24 branded (DATA — value objects with nominal type safety)
SnapshotManifest (DATA — cache metadata: version, atSequence, generatedAt, sourceEventCount)
CommitSha branded (DATA — branded string & {__brand: 'CommitSha'} for atopWhich dimension)
EditFunction (LOGIC — returns OntologyEdit[] without commit; disallowedTools enforces no side effects)
InterfaceType (LOGIC — shared behavioral contract; transition-zone reclassified from DATA)
LinkType / Object-Backed LinkType (LOGIC — relationship with/without properties; transition-zone reclassified from DATA)
DerivedProperty (LOGIC — computed, not stored; lazy-evaluated getter from codegen)
Reducer (LOGIC — fold over event collection; powers snapshot derivation Reducer<EventEnvelope, OntologySnapshot>)
LinkConstraint (LOGIC — cardinality / type / temporal constraint computation; returns Constraint[] not Edits[])
SubmissionCriteria computational (LOGIC — pre-flight evaluator; 9 constraint types: Range/ArraySize/StringLength/StringRegexMatch/OneOf/ObjectQueryResult/ObjectPropertyValue/GroupMember/Unevaluable)
ValidationPhaseEvaluator (LOGIC — 6-phase Palantir pipeline; v0 implements 3: Design+Compile / Runtime / Post-Write; each returns Pass | Fail<ErrorClass>)
Tier-1 Declarative Action (ACTION — pure CRUD rule compiled to commit_edits fast path; mutually exclusive with Tier-2)
Tier-2 Function-Backed Action (ACTION — wraps EditFunction with AtomicCommit; mutually exclusive with Tier-1)
AtomicCommit (ACTION — writes edits + appends edit_committed event in single mutex; primary: proper-lockfile; fallback: zero-dep fs.mkdirSync empirically proven)
ActionExecutorAgent (ACTION — plugin subagent with Write/Edit/Bash allowlist; sole agent permitted to commit)
CodegenRun (ACTION — descender regen triggered by PostToolUse(Edit schemas/ontology/*); appends codegen_started + codegen_completed events)
Layer-1 RBAC Policy (SECURITY — managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json per project; governs tools/paths/MCP allowlists; Layer-1 only in v0)
SubmissionCriteria gate semantics (SECURITY — dual model of LOGIC prim; gate that USES evaluation to permit or deny commit; policy review targets this)
CapabilityToken / Vault (SECURITY — ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/vault/ survives plugin updates; codegen emits token references, not secret values)
AppendOnlyEventLog events.jsonl (LEARN — per-project immutable NDJSON truth source; IS the Anthropic Managed Agents Session primitive scoped per-project)
DecisionLineage5Dim (LEARN — WHEN/ATOP_WHICH/THROUGH_WHICH/BY_WHOM/WITH_WHAT captured on every EventEnvelopeBase; root-cause analysis foundation)
EvidenceSufficiency (LEARN — meta-evaluation: sufficient | partial | insufficient; drives evaluator R9 gate)
LineageReplay (LEARN — MCP replay_lineage handler; deterministic replay of events.jsonl filtered by 5-dim query; foundation for BackwardProp)
DriftSignal (LEARN — drift_detected EventEnvelope variant: schema_mismatch | stale_codegen | orphan_reference; plugin monitor detects and emits)

data.name: EventEnvelope
data.description: The append-only Session log record. 10 variants via type discriminator. Immutable once appended. Every write to ontology state MUST flow through an EventEnvelope. Base carries Decision Lineage 5-dim: when, atopWhich, throughWhich, byWhom, withWhat.

data.name: ObjectType
data.description: Palantir ObjectType declaration with branded ObjectTypeRid. Each ObjectType holds stored-fact properties only (no derived). Compiled from ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/primitives/object-type.ts to src/generated/objects/*.ts per project.

data.name: PropertyType (24 branded)
data.description: 24 branded primitive property types from ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/types.ts extended with RID branding for OSDK 2.0 compliance. Value objects — no identity.

data.name: SnapshotManifest
data.description: Derived snapshot metadata: version, atSequence (replay cut-point), generatedAt, sourceEventCount. Snapshots are cache, not truth — events.jsonl is truth.

data.name: CommitSha (branded)
data.description: Branded string & { readonly __brand: 'CommitSha' } for atopWhich lineage dimension. Nominal type prevents mixing with SessionId / EventId at compile time.

logic.name: EditFunction
logic.description: Tier-2 function that takes params and returns Edits[] WITHOUT committing. Mechanically enforced via disallowedTools: [Write, Edit] on the edit-function agent .md frontmatter. Return type: OntologyEdit[] (Edits.Object | Edits.Link | Edits.Interface). Authoring helper does NOT commit edits per §LOGIC.FN-04.

logic.name: InterfaceType
logic.description: Shared behavioral contract across 2+ ObjectTypes. Transition-zone reclassification: enables polymorphic reasoning, not entity identity. LOGIC not DATA per ontologist SH-01.

logic.name: LinkType / Object-Backed LinkType
logic.description: Relationship between ObjectTypes. Plain LinkType when the relationship carries no properties; Object-Backed LinkType when it does. Transition-zone reclassification: enables reasoning/traversal, not stored fact.

logic.name: DerivedProperty
logic.description: Property computed from other properties. Not stored. Lives in schemas/ontology/functions/derived-property.ts. SH-01: if you delete the computation, raw data remains but derived value vanishes.

logic.name: Reducer
logic.description: Aggregation or fold over a collection of objects. Critical for snapshot derivation from events.jsonl: Reducer<EventEnvelope, OntologySnapshot> folds the log into current state. Powers LEARN -> DATA derivation per DC5-06.

logic.name: LinkConstraint
logic.description: Computed constraint on a LinkType (cardinality, type-of-both-ends, temporal ordering). Returns Constraint[] or boolean, no side effects.

logic.name: SubmissionCriteria (computational)
logic.description: Pre-flight business-logic evaluator. Returns Pass | Fail with 9 constraint classes (Range/ArraySize/StringLength/StringRegexMatch/OneOf/ObjectQueryResult/ObjectPropertyValue/GroupMember/Unevaluable). Mirrors OSDK 2.0 $validateOnly. Dual-modeled with prim-sec-02 (gate semantics).

logic.name: ValidationPhaseEvaluator
logic.description: 6-phase validation pipeline evaluator (Design/Compile/Deploy/Merge/Runtime/Post-Write per §VAL.R-03..R-08). v0 implements minimum 3: Design+Compile / Runtime / Post-Write. Phase-to-hook: Design+Compile -> PostToolUse(Edit schemas/ontology/*) bun tsc; Runtime -> commit_edits pre-flight; Post-Write -> PostToolUse(Edit) drift check.

action.name: Tier-1 Declarative Action
action.description: Pure CRUD rule. Declarative spec: given ObjectTypeX and field Y, on operation Z, apply rule R. No custom code path. Compiled into commit_edits handler fast path. Mutually exclusive with Tier-2 per §ACTION.MU-09..11.

action.name: Tier-2 Function-Backed Action
action.description: Action type that wraps an EditFunction (LOGIC) with an atomic commit. EditFunction computes Edits[] without committing; Tier-2 Action takes those Edits[] and commits them atomically with submission criteria pre-flight. Mutually exclusive with Tier-1.

action.name: AtomicCommit
action.description: The actual mutation: writes edits to ontology state AND appends edit_committed event to events.jsonl in a single atomic operation. Primary: proper-lockfile (mkdir-based NFS-safe). Fallback: zero-dep fs.mkdirSync empirically proven at 2x1000 events with 0 lost events (T7 prototype, 2 independent runs).

action.name: ActionExecutorAgent
action.description: Plugin subagent with Write/Edit/Bash allowlist. The ONLY palantir-mini agent permitted to commit. All other agents have disallowedTools covering Write/Edit. Per OMC architect.md pattern + Correction 1 (plugin agents cannot define hooks — hooks live at plugin-level hooks/hooks.json).

action.name: CodegenRun
action.description: Descender regeneration action triggered by PostToolUse(Edit schemas/ontology/*) hook. Reads ontology declarations, writes src/generated/* per project, appends codegen_started + codegen_completed events to events.jsonl.

security.name: Layer-1 RBAC Policy
security.description: Per-project managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json fragment declares permission allowlists (tools, paths, MCP servers). Layer 1 only in v0. Markings/ObjectSecurity/PermissionsCascade explicitly deferred to future phase.

security.name: SubmissionCriteria (gate semantics)
security.description: Pre-flight gate that blocks commits unless business-logic conditions pass. SECURITY dual of prim-logic-07. LOGIC evaluates pass/fail; SECURITY is the gate that USES the evaluation to permit or deny the commit. Intentional dual modeling per DC5 DDD.

security.name: CapabilityToken / Vault
security.description: Secrets live in ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/vault/ — survives plugin updates — referenced by capability token, never embedded in generated code. Mirrors Anthropic Managed Agents Vault proxy pattern.

learn.name: AppendOnlyEventLog (events.jsonl)
learn.description: Per-project <project>/.palantir-mini/session/events.jsonl. Append-only. Immutable lines. Each line is a serialized EventEnvelope. IS the Anthropic Managed Agents Session primitive scoped per-project. Three-source convergence: Anthropic MA, Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim, OMC clawhip event routing.

learn.name: DecisionLineage5Dim
learn.description: Palantir Decision Lineage 5 dimensions: WHEN, ATOP_WHICH, THROUGH_WHICH, BY_WHOM, WITH_WHAT. Captured on every EventEnvelope via EventEnvelopeBase. Enables root-cause analysis of ontology changes.

learn.name: EvidenceSufficiency
learn.description: Meta-evaluation: sufficient | partial | insufficient. Used by evaluator R9 gate. Drives researcher re-spawn for insufficient evidence gaps.

learn.name: LineageReplay
learn.description: Deterministic replay of events.jsonl filtered by 5-dim lineage. Given a filter (e.g. all events byWhom.agentName=ontologist in the last 24h), returns ordered event stream. Foundation for BackwardProp. Mirrors replay_lineage MCP tool.

learn.name: DriftSignal
learn.description: drift_detected EventEnvelope variant: schema_mismatch | stale_codegen | orphan_reference. Surfaces when runtime usage diverges from ontology declarations. Plugin drift-watch monitor detects and emits.

recommendedStack.name: TypeScript + Bun + Claude Code v2.1.108 Native Runtime + Append-Only Event Log (H-A + H-C)

technologies.name: TypeScript 5.x + Bun 1.3.x
strengths: TypeScript compile-time validation catches ontology schema regressions after codegen (§VAL.R-03)
Bun native test runner empirically confirmed: H-A prototype 10/10 clean at 2287 expect() calls in 5.64s
Bun speed enables practical PreCompact decision:block hook (fast tsc --noEmit)
Branded RID types prevent cross-domain confusion at zero runtime cost
weaknesses: proper-lockfile Bun compatibility is inferred (tier-3 claim, confidence 0.75); empirically resolved by fs.mkdirSync fallback in T7

technologies.name: Claude Code v2.1.108 Native Runtime
strengths: 24 hook events including PreCompact decision:block (v2.1.105), TaskCreated/TaskCompleted (v2.1.84), PostToolUseFailure — all required hooks exist
Agent Teams + custom subagents with disallowedTools enforce LOGIC/ACTION separation mechanically
Plugin manifest monitors field auto-arms drift-watch at session start
hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision (not deprecated top-level 'decision') verified for PreToolUse per Correction 2
${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} persists across plugin updates; vault pattern safe
managed-settings.d/ fragments enable per-project RBAC without modifying global settings
weaknesses: Plugin-shipped agents cannot define hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode — hooks must be at plugin-level (Correction 1)
API-Free constraint: no Managed Agents API, no --bare flag, no Bedrock/Vertex

technologies.name: Append-Only Event Log (events.jsonl + fs.mkdirSync mutex)
strengths: Empirically proven: 2x1000 events, 0 lost events, 0 torn writes, 0 duplicate sequences (T7 H-A prototype, 2 independent runs)
fs.mkdirSync mutex is zero-dependency; EEXIST is atomic on all filesystems including NFS
Three-source convergence (Anthropic MA, Palantir DL, OMC clawhip) — strongest external validation under API-Free constraint
Enables Decision Lineage 5-dim capture on every mutation as first-class record
BackwardProp path (replay_lineage -> lineage graph -> drift -> refinement) is structurally sound
weaknesses: O(n) replay cost for snapshot derivation — mitigated by SnapshotManifest cache at atSequence cut-points
proper-lockfile (backup mechanism) last published 2020; production path uses zero-dep fs.mkdirSync

technologies.name: OSDK 2.0-pattern Type Codegen
strengths: Edits discriminated union (Edits.Object<T>, Edits.Interface<T>, Edits.Link<Src,'linkName'>) enables exhaustive compile-time coverage
createEditBatch<OntologyEdit>(client) pattern: batch.getEdits() — reading property after update does NOT reflect uncommitted change (safe for preview semantics)
palantir-math data.ts (141KB, 2190 LOC) refactored via primitives/ split to fix OSDK 1.x single-file threshold anti-pattern
weaknesses: Foundry edit functions require wrapping as function-backed Actions to commit — commit_edits MCP fills this role in palantir-mini

recommendedStack.rationale: H-A (Append-Only Event Log) wins across 3 independent evaluation axes: (1) ARCHITECTURAL CONVERGENCE — three independent references (Anthropic Managed Agents 'Session=append-only log', Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim §DL-02, OMC clawhip event routing) converge on the append-only log pattern; (2) EMPIRICAL SUPERIORITY — H-A prototype: 0 lost events / 2000 (0 torn writes, 0 duplicate sequences, 2 independent runs) vs H-B: 484/2000 lost updates (24.2%, TOCTOU structural confirmed across 3 runs, 56-484 range); (3) ONTOLOGY FIT — H-A populates all 5 domains first-class (28 primitives, overallForwardPropStatus=healthy, overallBackwardPropStatus=healthy) while H-B cannot reliably populate LEARN (R11 triggered) and has structurally broken BackwardProp (R13 triggered). H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) accepted as co-winner — orthogonal to H-A, runs ON TOP of event-log substrate.

alternatives.name: H-B: Snapshot + StaleObject optimistic concurrency
alternatives.whyNot: Empirical defeat: 484/2000 lost updates (24.2%) under 2-writer adversarial race across 3 independent runs. TOCTOU window is structural not load-dependent. Destroys Decision Lineage 5-dim — prim-learn-01 BackwardProp structurally impossible from H-B substrate. Triggered R11 (LEARN D/L/A Fit broken) and R13 (BackwardProp broken) on evaluator gate. Preserved as formal competitor of record.

alternatives.name: Full 6-phase Palantir validation pipeline
alternatives.whyNot: Deploy/Merge phases presume Foundry-like branching workflow absent in Claude Code Native Runtime. Git already handles merge-time validation via normal branch workflows. v0 correct endpoint is 3-phase (Design+Compile / Runtime / Post-Write) per H-C — extensible to 6-phase post-v0 via OCP.

forwardProp.description: ForwardProp: ontology declarations -> palantir-mini codegen -> per-project generated code -> project runtime -> events.jsonl append -> derived snapshot. 6 steps, all healthy per world-model T4 audit (overallForwardPropStatus=healthy).
steps: Step 1 (ontology declarations): ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/{primitives,functions,policies,lineage,generators}/ — TypeScript with branded RIDs; DDD: matches Palantir OSDK 2.0 vocabulary; DRY: single schema shared across 3 projects

Step 2 (palantir-mini codegen): ~/.claude/plugins/palantir-mini/lib/codegen/descender-gen.ts + client-generated-split.ts — OCP: adding primitives adds generator templates, never modifies existing generators
Step 3 (per-project generated code): <project>/src/generated/{objects,links,actions,functions,events}.d.ts — OSDK 2.0 client/generated split; generated code never hand-edited
Step 4 (project runtime): <project>/ TypeScript importing src/generated/ — PECS: consumers use supertypes, producers extend; edit functions / action executors / UI surfaces are strongly typed
Step 5 (events.jsonl append): <project>/.palantir-mini/session/events.jsonl — DRY: single append path for all state changes via AtomicCommit + fs.mkdirSync mutex
Step 6 (derived snapshot): <project>/.palantir-mini/session/snapshots/{ontology,lineage,manifest}.json — cached fold of events.jsonl via Reducer primitive; snapshot is cache, events.jsonl is truth

backwardProp.description: BackwardProp: runtime events -> replay_lineage MCP -> 5-dim lineage graph -> refinement proposal -> ontologist review -> ontology update. 6 steps; steps 4+5 have minor persistence gaps deferred to post-v0. overallBackwardPropStatus=healthy.
steps: Step 1 (runtime events): <project>/.palantir-mini/session/events.jsonl — append-only capturing all 5 Decision Lineage dimensions per envelope; DDD: WHEN/ATOP_WHICH/THROUGH_WHICH/BY_WHOM/WITH_WHAT

Step 2 (replay_lineage MCP tool): ~/.claude/plugins/palantir-mini/bridge/handlers/replay-lineage.ts — MCP handler reads events.jsonl filtered by 5-dim query; DRY: single replay primitive serves all backward queries
Step 3 (decision lineage graph): in-memory materialization from replay output — 5-dim graph (DDD: matches Palantir Decision Lineage vocabulary)

Step 4 (refinement proposal): ontologist agent inbox via SendMessage + decision-log append — non-durable in v0; post-v0: add refinement_proposed EventEnvelope variant; PECS: proposal consumers use supertype
Step 5 (ontologist review): ontologist agent accepts|rejects|revises; decision logged in decision-log.json — human-in-the-loop governance; post-v0: add review_decision event variant
Step 6 (ontology declaration update): ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ git commit — triggers full ForwardProp cycle; closed loop: BackwardProp terminates at start of ForwardProp
gaps: Step 4 (refinement proposal persistence): non-durable in v0 (SendMessage + decision-log append only). Post-v0: add refinement_proposed variant to EventEnvelope union.
Step 5 (ontologist review decision): should itself be a LEARN-domain event. Post-v0: add review_decision variant to EventEnvelope union.

implementationStrategy.name: Golden Tables
implementationStrategy.description: Establish events.jsonl per project as the durable Golden Table. Publish ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ as stable typed interface (the OSDK). palantir-math migrates first (most mature, highest urgency with 141KB data.ts anti-pattern).
deliverables: ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ initial publication: event-types.ts + primitives/ + functions/ + policies/ + generators/
Plugin scaffold: .claude-plugin/{plugin.json,marketplace.json} + hooks/hooks.json + bridge/mcp-server.ts + agents/*.md
EventEnvelope discriminated union (10 variants + EventEnvelopeBase with 5-dim lineage) in schemas/ontology/lineage/event-types.ts
palantir-mini codegen/descender-gen.ts initial implementation targeting palantir-math data.ts refactor
palantir-math migration Steps 1-3 (schema.ts 2.5KB, security.ts 4KB, data.ts 141KB split into primitives/)
AppendOnlyEventLog library: lib/event-log/{append.ts,read.ts,replay.ts} with fs.mkdirSync mutex (zero-dep)
Bun test suite for event-log (H-A prototype as seed: 10 cases, 2287 expect() calls verified clean)
Per-project managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json Layer-1 RBAC fragments
dependencies: schemas/ontology/ initial publication must precede codegen implementation
codegen must produce valid tsc-clean output for palantir-math before Step 3 migration
fs.mkdirSync fallback (not proper-lockfile) as primary atomic-append mechanism per T7 empirical verdict

implementationStrategy.name: Operational Decision-Making
implementationStrategy.description: Enable two-tier actions + submission criteria + 3-phase validation pipeline. Edit functions return Edits[] without commit. Action executor wraps with atomic commit. MCP bridge exposes 5 tools. palantir-math migration Steps 4-7. kosmos and mathcrew migration.
deliverables: 5 MCP tools in bridge/mcp-server.ts: get_ontology / emit_event / apply_edit_function / commit_edits / replay_lineage
lib/actions/{commit.ts,tier1-declarative.ts,tier2-function.ts} with SubmissionCriteria pre-flight evaluator
agents/action-executor.md (Write/Edit/Bash allowlist), agents/edit-function.md (disallowedTools: Write/Edit/Bash)
hooks/hooks.json: 3-phase validation (Design+Compile PostToolUse / Runtime commit_edits / Post-Write drift check), hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision format
monitors/monitors.json: drift-watch monitor for schema_mismatch | stale_codegen | orphan_reference
palantir-math migration Steps 4-7: capabilities.ts (57KB) -> rbac.ts, changeContracts.ts (66KB) -> tier1+tier2, events.jsonl wire-up, compat shim
kosmos migration: ontology-state/*.json backed by events.jsonl; decision-log events emitted on pipeline phase completion
mathcrew migration: event emission for content delivery and photonic realism sessions
dependencies: Phase 1 Golden Tables must be stable before Phase 2 action layer
palantir-math Step 3 (data.ts refactor) must complete before Step 5 (capabilities migration)
hooks/hooks.json must use hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision for PreToolUse (Correction 2 from source-map)

implementationStrategy.name: AI-First
implementationStrategy.description: MCP bridge exposes operations for Claude/Codex/Gemini. ontology-verifier agent proactively detects drift. replay_lineage surfaces real refinement signals. BackwardProp loop fully closes with refinement_proposed + review_decision event variants.
deliverables: ontology-verifier custom subagent: reads events.jsonl + schemas/ontology/, emits drift_detected events
Cross-project BackwardProp: kosmos can call replay_lineage against palantir-math events.jsonl
refinement_proposed EventEnvelope variant (closes BackwardProp step-4 gap)
review_decision EventEnvelope variant (closes BackwardProp step-5 gap)
CapabilityToken vault population: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/vault/*.json for any MCP bridge credentials
Historical session lineage: prior sessions (jsxgraph-sequencer, mathcrew-content-delivery, mathcrew-photonic-realism) loaded as replay-only events.jsonl
Progressive autonomy gates: AIP Automate-style staged review using submission_criteria_passed + decision lineage as autonomy evidence
dependencies: Phase 2 MCP bridge must be stable + tested before Phase 3 cross-project use
BackwardProp steps 4+5 gap fills require Phase 1+2 events.jsonl to have accumulated sufficient signal
ontology-verifier subagent must not define hooks (enforced by CC plugin restrictions)

evaluatorReason: All 15 rejection criteria applied (R1-R15). 0 rules triggered on winning hypothesis H-A + co-winner H-C. 2 rules triggered on losing hypothesis H-B: R11 (LEARN D/L/A Fit — 24.2% empirical update loss makes LEARN domain unreliable on H-B substrate) and R13 (BackwardProp structurally broken — no event history to replay after snapshot overwrite). These R11/R13 triggers are pipeline health signals confirming the evaluator gate correctly discriminates real architectural quality — they strengthen H-A's win, not weaken the pipeline. H-A: passRate=1.0 (10/10), 0 failureModes, adversarialRaceResult 0/2000 lost events (2 independent runs). H-B: passRate=1.0 (10/10), 4 failureModes (2 critical: LEARN+ACTION; 2 high: LOGIC+DATA). winRationale is specific and non-generic (3 enumerated axes). 0 debate rounds used (out of max 2) — evidence chain is coherent. whatWouldChangeDecision has 5 concrete reversal conditions. ACCEPT.

H-A.label: Append-Only Event Log Foundation
H-A.keyFinding: Zero-dep fs.mkdirSync mutex proven sufficient for concurrent append safety. EventEnvelope discriminated union provides compile-time exhaustiveness. foldToSnapshot() Reducer confirms O(n) LEARN-domain BackwardProp pattern.

H-B.label: Snapshot-based + StaleObject optimistic concurrency

failureModes.description: 24.2% empirical update loss (484/2000). AppendOnlyEventLog cannot be populated from a lossy substrate. BackwardProp structurally impossible.
TOCTOU window between read-check-write is non-atomic. Two writers can both pass version check simultaneously, producing silent lost updates.
StaleObjectError detection bypassed in true OS-level concurrency when two writers enter the TOCTOU window simultaneously. Coverage incomplete.
No lineage record across versions. Each write overwrites prior state; prior states irrecoverable from disk after overwrite.

H-B.keyFinding: TOCTOU weakness is structural not load-dependent: 56-484 lost updates across 3 independent runs. H-B preserved as formal competitor of record.

winRationale: H-A (Append-Only Event Log Foundation) is the decisive winner across 3 independent evaluation axes: (1) ARCHITECTURAL CONVERGENCE — three independent references (Anthropic Managed Agents 'Session = append-only log of everything that happened', Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim §DL-02/§DL-09, OMC clawhip 'event routing to prevent context pollution') all converge on the append-only log pattern — strongest evidence class under API-Free constraint; (2) EMPIRICAL SUPERIORITY — H-A prototype passes 2-writer adversarial race at 2x1000 events with 0 duplicate sequences, 0 torn writes, 0 lost events (reproducible across 2 independent runs) versus H-B losing 484/2000 updates (24.2%, confirmed structural TOCTOU across 3 runs, 56-484 range); (3) ONTOLOGY FIT — H-A populates all 5 domains first-class per world-model's 28 primitives (DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/SECURITY/LEARN all healthy) while H-B cannot reliably populate LEARN (R11 triggered) and has structurally broken BackwardProp (R13 triggered). H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) accepted as co-winner: it is orthogonal to H-A vs H-B and runs ON TOP of the event-log substrate. ACCEPT.

alternatives.whyNot: Snapshot + StaleObject optimistic concurrency: empirically defeated at 484/2000 lost updates (24.2% loss rate) in 2-writer adversarial race. TOCTOU window is structural (56-484 range across 3 independent runs). Destroys Decision Lineage 5-dim — BackwardProp from H-B substrate is structurally impossible. Triggered evaluator R11 (LEARN D/L/A Fit broken) and R13 (BackwardProp path broken). Works for single-writer sequential systems but palantir-mini LEARN centrality is non-negotiable.

whatWouldChangeDecision: If a v1 integration test shows fs.mkdirSync fallback is insufficient under 10+ concurrent writers AND proper-lockfile is empirically broken under Bun, revisit H-A's atomic append mechanism (application-level retry-on-conflict or alternative lockfile strategy).
If H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) fails integration in v1 due to PostToolUse hook latency burnout, reconsider validation monitoring scope (retreat to 2-phase or move to async:true).
If a new MCP server pattern emerges that makes snapshot-based architecture viable for LEARN (e.g., CRDT-based merging with cross-version history), reconsider H-B or H-A+snapshot hybrid.
If palantir-math migration reveals EventEnvelope variant gaps that require breaking schema bumps before v0 stabilizes, revisit gap-04 and consider versioned events.jsonl.
If cross-project write coordination (gap-03) proves insufficient because Codex/Gemini become writers not readers, reintroduce multi-writer coordination mechanism.
